pages.title: About – Chris Law
pages.meta_description: Chris Law MP has been the Member of Parliament for the constituency of Dundee Central, previously Dundee West, since his election in the 2015 General Election. He ha since been re-elected in 2017, 2019 and 2024. The Dundee Central seat encompasses the Dundee City Council wards of Lochee, the West End, Strathmartine, Coldside and Maryfield,…
pages.text_excerpt: About – Chris Law Skip to content Chris Law MP for Dundee Central Facebook Twitter Instagram Menu Home About Westminster Voting Record Speeches Supported Early Day Motions Dundee Contact e-Newsletter More About Chris Law MP has been the Member of Parliament for the constituency of Dundee Central, previously Dundee West, since his election in the 2015 General Election. He ha since been re-elected in 2017, 2019 and 2024. The Dundee Central seat encompasses the Dundee City Council wards of Lochee, the West End, Strathmartine, Coldside and Maryfield, with some parts of the city’s East End ward and a very small part of The Ferry ward also included. Westminster Parliament currently has powers over areas including defence and foreign affairs, broadcasting, International Development, energy policy, Universal Credit and Child Support, fiscal and economic policy, social security including pensions, immigration and passports. You can find out more about what is and isn’t reserved here . You can find Chris’s constituency office at the bottom of Lochee High Street. Chris is the SNP’s Westminster Business, Trade and Development Spokesperson. He previously sat on the Scottish Affairs Select Committee from 2015-2017, and on the International Development Committee from 2017-2024. He chairs the All Party Parliamentary Group (APPG) for Guantanamo Bay and the APPG for Tibet. Prior to his election, Chris Law ran the Spirit of Independence Campaign with his Green Goddess Fire Engine. He previously studied as a French Chef, and graduated with a degree in Cultural and Social Anthropology from the University of St Andrews. He has previously run his own businesses, including motorbike tours of the Himalayas and as a mortgage adviser in Dundee. pages.title: MP backs Fashion Watchdog campaign at Dundee Soroptimist conference – Chris Law
pages.h1: MP backs Fashion Watchdog campaign at Dundee Soroptimist conference
pages.meta_description: Dundee West MP Chris Law has backed the growing campaign for a Fashion Watchdog, following calls for support for the Transform Trade campaign by Scottish Soroptimists. Members of Soroptimist International met at Dundee’s Discovery Point on Saturday 3rd June to discuss issues around fast fashion and how communities can collectively transform the fashion trade to…
pages.text_excerpt: MP backs Fashion Watchdog campaign at Dundee Soroptimist conference – Chris Law Skip to content Chris Law MP for Dundee Central Facebook Twitter Instagram Menu Home About Westminster Voting Record Speeches Supported Early Day Motions Dundee Contact e-Newsletter More MP backs Fashion Watchdog campaign at Dundee Soroptimist conference June 5, 2023 chrislawsnp Dundee West MP Chris Law has backed the growing campaign for a Fashion Watchdog, following calls for support for the Transform Trade campaign by Scottish Soroptimists. Members of Soroptimist International met at Dundee’s Discovery Point on Saturday 3 rd June to discuss issues around fast fashion and how communities can collectively transform the fashion trade to work for all involved. As part of the conference, members handed over crafted handmade dogs to Chris Law for delivery to Scottish MPs who have not yet backed the campaign. A Fashion Watchdog, initially proposed in the Fashion Supply Chain Adjudicator Bill, would ensure that fashion companies were held responsible for mistreatment of suppliers and workers, and encourage more ethical behaviour in the wider industry. Fifty-nine Members of Parliament have so far backed Transform Trade’s campaign and signed a pledge to support a Fashion Watchdog. Dundee West MP Chris Law said: “It was fantastic to welcome members of Soroptimist International to my city of Dundee and to take part in just some of their day’s conference on the global issues around fast fashion. “I have been proud to support the campaign for a Fashion Watchdog, which seeks to ensure a fairer fashion industry for all involved, and I will be backing efforts in Parliament to deliver this. “Campaigns like this one by Transform Trade and supported by Soroptimist International are vital in raising awareness of the need for further oversight of damaging practices in the fashion industry.” Anne McAlister and Aileen Eland, from Soroptimist International, said: “We are delighted to support the Transform Trade campaign for a Fashion Watchdog for the UK. We have heard today about the issues around the garment industry and the need for change that values people over profit. Soroptimists will continue to work together to make a difference.” Share this: Share on X (Opens in new window) X Share on Facebook (Opens in new window) Facebook Like Loading...
